text_original: The latter, however intelligent might be his mind, felt it necessary to set forth and obtain a decision on what his object was;-showing that he did not dare to take his own way, and giving honour to Heaven (as the supreme Decider).
text_normalized: The latter, however intelligent might be his mind, felt it necessary to set forth and obtain a decision on what his object was;-showing that he did not dare to take his own way, and giving honour to Heaven (as the supreme Decider).

text_original: What was good in him (or in his views) he ascribed to others
text_normalized: What was good in him (or in his views) he ascribed to others

text_original: what was wrong, to himself
text_normalized: what was wrong, to himself

text_original: thus teaching not to boast, and giving honour to men of talents and virtue.
text_normalized: thus teaching not to boast, and giving honour to men of talents and virtue.

text_original: When a filial son was about to sacrifice
text_normalized: When a filial son was about to sacrifice

text_original: the rule was that he should have his mind well adjusted and grave, to fit him for giving to all matters their full consideration, for providing the robes and other things, for repairing the temple and its fanes, and for regulating everything.
text_normalized: the rule was that he should have his mind well adjusted and grave, to fit him for giving to all matters their full consideration, for providing the robes and other things, for repairing the temple and its fanes, and for regulating everything.

text_original: When the day of sacrifice arrived, the rule was that his countenance should be mild, and his movements show an anxious dread, as if he feared his love were not sufficient.
text_normalized: When the day of sacrifice arrived, the rule was that his countenance should be mild, and his movements show an anxious dread, as if he feared his love were not sufficient.

text_original: When he put down his offerings, it was required that his demeanour should be mild, and his body bent, as if (his parents) would speak (to him) and had not yet done so
text_normalized: When he put down his offerings, it was required that his demeanour should be mild, and his body bent, as if (his parents) would speak (to him) and had not yet done so

text_original: when the officers assisting had all gone out, he stood lowly and still, though correct and straight, as if he were about to lose the sight (of his parents).
text_normalized: when the officers assisting had all gone out, he stood lowly and still, though correct and straight, as if he were about to lose the sight (of his parents).

text_original: After the sacrifice, he looked pleased and expectant, as if they would again enter.
text_normalized: After the sacrifice, he looked pleased and expectant, as if they would again enter.

text_original: In this way his ingenuousness and goodness were never absent from his person
text_normalized: In this way his ingenuousness and goodness were never absent from his person

text_original: his ears and eyes were never withdrawn from what was in his heart
text_normalized: his ears and eyes were never withdrawn from what was in his heart

text_original: the exercises of his thoughts never left his parents.
text_normalized: the exercises of his thoughts never left his parents.

text_original: What was bound up in his heart was manifested in his countenance
text_normalized: What was bound up in his heart was manifested in his countenance

text_original: and he was continually examining himself;-such was the mind of the filial son.
text_normalized: and he was continually examining himself;-such was the mind of the filial son.

text_original: The sites for the altars to the spirits of the land and grain were on the right
text_normalized: The sites for the altars to the spirits of the land and grain were on the right

text_original: that for the ancestral temple on the left.
text_normalized: that for the ancestral temple on the left.
